title: Review: fix: apply review findings from nie-sez5 (and predecessors)
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — Two real issues in the JMAP layer (atomicity violation, test oracle gap) and one in Android lifecycle handling. Nothing catastrophic. The atomicity issue (nie-sjc4.1) is the only one that could cause silent data divergence in production.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) did not run tests — cargo test would confirm the test oracle weakness is real; (2) did not read full JMAP HTTP handler or store schema to verify the atomicity rollback path fully; (3) Android lifecycle is platform-specific and hard to reason about from code alone. Would raise confidence if: cargo test --workspace passes clean, and the space_set handler is instrumented with a failing test for the mixed-patch atomicity case.
